clinical_history: This ECG from a 41 year old woman status post a sudden cardiac arrest and ICD placement is most suggestive of which ONE of the following?
diagnosis: The ECG shows sinus rhythm at a rate of about 60/min. Intervals and QRS axis are normal. The recording is notable for notched Q waves in leads V2 and V3 and prominent narrow Q waves (as QR complexes) in V4-V6. The lateral T waves are tall and symmetrical. The pattern resembles an anterior MI. Pseudo-infarct patterns (especially with narrow, deep Q waves) are not uncommon with hypertrophic cardiomyopathy. Echocardiogram showed normal overall left ventricular wall motion with classic asymmetric septal hypertrophy. There was a resting left ventricular outflow gradient of 15 mmHg, increasing to 18 mmHg during the strain phase of the Valsalva maneuver.
